name: robo-cortex
description: Git-aware, long-term memory for LLM coding agents working inside a git repository.
long_description: robo-cortex is a local knowledge base for AI coding agents. Memories are anchored to git blob hashes, so when the code they describe changes, the memory is automatically flagged for review instead of being silently trusted or silently lost. No embeddings, no vector database, no cloud dependency — a local SQLite store scoped to your repository.

tools.name: retrieve_context
tools.description: Compact, ranked, budget-honest context pack for a task — the primary read path an agent calls before starting work.

tools.name: record_memory
tools.description: The only way new memories enter the store; linked paths are validated against HEAD before the row is written.

tools.name: attach_evidence
tools.description: Strengthen an existing memory with provenance, ideally re-runnable.

tools.name: get_memory
tools.description: Full detail on one memory: fields, evidence, links, and (with a task) its retrieval score breakdown.

tools.name: change_status
tools.description: The only way a memory reaches a deliberate final state (superseded, invalidated, abandoned, archived), or is manually restored to active.

tools.name: search_memory
tools.description: Exploration and debugging over the memory store — broader and less strictly budgeted than retrieve_context.

tools.name: list_affected
tools.description: What a diff puts at risk — the proactive half of invalidation, for use before or right after a change.

tools.name: verify_evidence
tools.description: The single, explicit re-verification entry point for evidence, including Gitea-backed evidence.

repo_path.title: Repository path
repo_path.description: The git repository robo-cortex should read and write memory for. Leave empty to use the agent's current working directory.
